title: Upload & Convert — How to use
subtitle: Convert amount columns across multiple sheets from one currency to another using historical or monthly exchange rates.

sections.title: Step 1 — Upload your file
items: Click the upload area or drag-and-drop an Excel (.xlsx / .xls) or CSV file onto it.
Files with multiple sheets are fully supported — each sheet is processed independently.
Sheets that contain no numeric amount columns are flagged automatically and skipped during conversion.

sections.title: Step 2 — Confirm the header row
items: The app automatically detects which row contains column names. Review the detected header row and its column chips.
If the header is wrong, click 'Incorrect' and pick the correct row from the candidate list — up to 4 data rows are shown so you can select further down the sheet.
Once you click 'Correct', the column mapping panel opens automatically.

sections.title: Step 3 — Map your columns
items: For each column the app will pre-select a type: Amount, Date, or No Action.
Amount columns: set the 'From' currency (original) and 'To' currency (target). The first currency you pick is automatically suggested for all other amount columns.
Date columns: the app uses the per-row date to look up the exact daily exchange rate for that day.
Period columns (e.g. 'Jan 2024'): if your amounts are organised by month rather than a date column, set the month/year for each column individually.
No Action: select this for columns like IDs, descriptions, or any column you don't want converted.
Aggregate columns (Total, Grand Total, Sub Total): these are automatically excluded from direct conversion and recomputed as the sum of the converted columns after conversion.

sections.title: Step 4 — Confirm and convert
items: Tick the 'I confirm the mappings above are correct' checkbox at the bottom once all sheets are configured.
Click 'Confirm & Convert' to fetch exchange rates and apply them. A spinner indicates the API call is in progress.
The converted data preview appears in the 'Converted Data' expansion panel beneath the mapping panel.

sections.title: Step 5 — Download the result
items: Click the download icon to get the converted Excel file.
The downloaded file is your original workbook with only the converted amount cells patched — all formatting, styles, merged cells, and other sheets are preserved exactly as uploaded.
Date values, metadata rows, and non-amount columns remain untouched in the output.

sections.title: Tips & edge cases
items: Sheets with no amount fields (e.g. notes or reference sheets) are shown with a notice and are copied as-is into the output without any changes.
If a column contains currency symbols in standalone cells (e.g. '$3,200,000') it is detected as an amount. Long prose sentences that merely mention a dollar amount are not treated as amount columns.
Exchange rates are fetched from the FetchXRates API. Make sure the selected date or period has available rate data.
Use the Reset button (left button in the action bar) to clear everything and start over with a new file.
